page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-80 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 80.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance characteristics, particularly in CPU-bound operations. The instance achieves top 25% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as Passmark CPU and physics tests. Cryptographic performance is highly efficient, with the Passmark CPU encryption test ranking in the top 10%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets. Memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 187.06 GB/sec for large block read-only operations. However, single-threaded text processing and decompression tasks fall into the average tier.

The primary tradeoff of the n2d-highcpu-80 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases or large-scale in-memory caching. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile is optimized for high-density compute workloads, allowing users to scale processing power without paying for unneeded memory capacity. This makes the instance well-suited for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, Redis operations, and moderate-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-80 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs and 80.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers strong multi-core performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng and Passmark CPU benchmarks. The hardware profile lacks local SSD storage and GPU accelerators, making it highly specialized for CPU-bound tasks. It exhibits exceptional cryptographic capabilities, placing in the top 10% for Passmark encryption tests due to hardware-accelerated instruction sets. With a low 1.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio, this instance offers a cost-efficient design for processing-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is highly suitable for software compilation, ray tracing, Redis operations, and parallelized compute tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 80 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 80 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-80 is a compute-optimized server with 80 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 80 GB RAM, ideal for CPU-heavy parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring eighty dedicated AMD EPYC cores optimized for parallel processing, cryptography, and software compilation.

Strong multi-core performance, ranking in the top 25% of stress-ng benchmarks
